name: Nickel/cobalt sensor (RcnR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects nickel and cobalt in water. The native E. coli RcnR metal-responsive repressor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible chromoprotein, in safe E. coli K-12.

input.detects: Bioavailable nickel and cobalt in water

sensing.sensorMechanism: RcnR represses the rcnAB efflux promoter (PrcnA) and is released when it binds Ni(II)/Co(II).
sensing.signalFlow: Ni/Co -> RcnR releases PrcnA -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> dCas9 + sgRNA repress a constitutive amilCP cassette (CRISPRi) -> pigment fades with metal (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

parts.name: RcnR regulator
parts.notes: Ni(II)/Co(II)-responsive repressor; endogenous to E. coli.

performance.notes: RcnR senses both nickel and cobalt; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained; add kill-switch for field use.
safety.notes: E. coli K-12 non-pathogenic BSL-1 host; instrument-free pigment readout.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PrcnA -> sgRNA (native RcnR). TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Select; confirm baseline pigment without metal.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add water sample + Ni/Co standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native E. coli RcnR nickel/cobalt sensor with CRISPRi
partsSources: Iwig et al. 2006 / RcnR nickel-cobalt sensing (E. coli)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
